subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Tunnel
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: One rainy evening, a boy named Ruskin was walking along the railway tracks. He loved adventures but sometimes acted carelessly. The sound of the train whistle filled the air as he approached a dark tunnel.
questions: What kind of evening was it?
What filled the air as he approached the tunnel?

passages.paragraph: Ruskin looked at the tunnel and felt a mix of excitement and fear. He knew that walking through the tunnel could be dangerous, but he wanted to see what was inside.
questions: What did Ruskin feel about the tunnel?
Why was he feeling this way?
Did he think the tunnel was safe?
What did he want to do?
What emotion did excitement mix with?

passages.paragraph: The rain poured heavily, making the ground slippery. Ruskin stepped carefully but his boots slipped on the wet stones. He realized that the tunnel would be even more difficult to cross than he had imagined.
questions: What did he realize about the tunnel?
Was crossing the tunnel easy for him?

passages.paragraph: Inside the tunnel, darkness surrounded Ruskin. The walls echoed with the sound of dripping water. He felt nervous but moved forward slowly, trying not to fall.
Suddenly, Ruskin heard the distant roar of a train. Panic rose in his chest. He knew he had to escape quickly. He ran as fast as he could, trying to find the light at the end of the tunnel.
questions: What did he know he had to do?
What was he trying to find?

passages.paragraph: His heart pounded as he slipped on the wet floor. Scratches appeared on his hands and legs, but he did not stop. Every step brought him closer to safety, and he focused on reaching the tunnel's end.
questions: What happened to his hands and legs?
Was he getting closer to safety?

passages.paragraph: Finally, Ruskin saw the faint light of the tunnel exit. Relief washed over him as he ran the last few steps. He had faced danger and learned how important it was to be careful.
questions: What did he do in the last few steps?

passages.paragraph: Outside the tunnel, the rain had stopped. Ruskin took a deep breath and looked back at the dark passage. He realized that adventure could be thrilling, but safety should always come first.
questions: What did he realize about adventure?

passages.paragraph: He promised himself that next time he would be more careful. Ruskin knew that being brave did not mean being reckless. Thoughtfulness and caution were also important on adventures.
questions: What did he understand about bravery?
Does being brave mean being reckless?
What qualities are important on adventures?
Will he act carelessly next time?

passages.paragraph: The story teaches that courage and adventure are valuable, but one must always take care and be aware of dangers. Ruskin returned home safely, wiser and more thoughtful after his experience in the tunnel.
questions: How did he feel after the experience?
